FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class SplitButton

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI 3 SplitButton: a primary action button joined to a secondary dropdown half, sharing one rounded chrome with a 1px divider between them. The two halves are independently interactive — the primary half runs OnInvoke (the Click event / OnClickPrimary), the secondary half opens a MenuFlyout anchored below the whole control (OnClickSecondary → OpenFlyout). Each half carries its own hover/press fill + foreground ramp; the inner corner where each half meets the divider is squared so the pair reads as one control (WinUI PrimaryButtonBorder 4,0,0,4 / SecondaryButtonBorder 0,4,4,0).

Source of truth: microsoft-ui-xaml/controls/dev/SplitButton/SplitButton.xaml (+ _themeresources.xaml, SplitButton.cpp). Tokens, sizes, padding, corners and the state matrix are 1:1 with that template.

public sealed class SplitButton : Component

Field Value

bool

PartChevron

The 12×12 chevron box inside the dropdown half (the AnimatedIcon column). Owned: none.

public const string PartChevron = "Chevron"

Field Value

string

PartDivider

The 1px divider between the halves (WinUI DividerBackgroundGrid). Owned: none.

public const string PartDivider = "Divider"

Field Value

string

PartPrimaryButton

The primary half (WinUI PrimaryButton). Owned: OnClick (OnInvoke), Role, Children (the PrimaryContent slot).

public const string PartPrimaryButton = "PrimaryButton"

Field Value

string

PartRoot

The joined chrome (WinUI RootGrid + Border): fill, border, corners, clip. Owned: OnKeyDown (the Space/Enter/Down/F4 chords), OnRealized (flyout anchor capture, chained), Children.

public const string PartRoot = "Root"

Field Value

string

PartSecondaryButton

The dropdown half (WinUI SecondaryButton). Owned: OnClick (the flyout toggle), Role, Children.

public const string PartSecondaryButton = "SecondaryButton"

Field Value

string

Parts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see TemplateParts for the contract.

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
